title: OAuthRefreshPolicyContract
description: Language-agnostic contract for the OAuth2 token model and the parameterized refresh-lead / expiry policy shared across forgecode (Rust), OmniRoute (TypeScript), and cliproxy (Go). Version: 1.0.0. This is the P5.2 expansion of the inline OAuthRefreshPolicy `$def` in provider-model.schema.json: it adds the full token-field model and per-provider override surface. forgecode's `forge_domain::auth::credentials::{AuthCredential, OAuthTokens}` is the reference implementation.

ProviderId.description: Provider identifier, snake_case for built-ins, e.g. 'anthropic', 'openai', 'codebuddy'. Matches ProviderId in provider-model.schema.json.

TokenType.description: OAuth token_type as returned by the token endpoint. 'Bearer' is the near-universal value.

OAuthTokens.description: The persisted OAuth2 token set for a single provider credential. Reference: forgecode `forge_domain::auth::credentials::OAuthTokens` (access_token, refresh_token, expires_at). The optional id_token / token_type / scope fields are part of the contract for cross-repo alignment (OmniRoute and cliproxy persist them); forgecode currently persists the required subset and MAY ignore the optional fields.

access_token.description: The bearer access token used to authenticate API requests. SECRET — never logged in cleartext.

refresh_token.description: The refresh token used to obtain a new access_token. SECRET. Null when the provider does not issue refresh tokens (e.g. short-lived ADC tokens that are re-minted rather than refreshed).

id_token.description: OIDC id_token (JWT) when the provider returns one. Optional; not all providers issue an id_token. SECRET.

expires_at.description: Absolute UTC timestamp (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601) at which the access_token expires. The refresh decision is computed against this instant, not against a relative expires_in. Implementations that receive a relative `expires_in` (seconds) MUST convert it to an absolute expires_at = now + expires_in at acquisition time (forgecode: `calculate_token_expiry` in forge_infra/auth/util.rs).

scope.description: Space-delimited list of granted OAuth scopes, as returned by the token endpoint. Null when the provider does not echo scopes.

RefreshLeadOverride.description: A per-provider override of the default refresh-lead. cliproxy varies the lead per provider (most 300 s; codebuddy 86400 s). Such overrides are CONTRACT-CONFORMANT precisely because the lead is parameterized.

refresh_lead_seconds.description: Seconds before expires_at at which this specific provider's token should be refreshed. Overrides default_refresh_lead_seconds for this provider only.

rationale.description: Why this provider deviates from the default lead, e.g. 'codebuddy issues 24h-lifetime tokens; refresh a full day ahead'.

OAuthRefreshPolicy.description: The parameterized refresh policy. The default lead (300 s = 5 min) is identical across all three repos; per-provider overrides are the parameterization point. The needs_refresh predicate is normative and identical across repos.

default_refresh_lead_seconds.description: Default seconds-before-expiry at which a refresh is triggered. 300 s matches forgecode `chrono::Duration::minutes(5)` (forge_services/src/provider_auth.rs), OmniRoute `TOKEN_EXPIRY_BUFFER = 5*60*1000` ms, and cliproxy `5 * time.Minute` for the majority of providers.

refresh_lead_overrides.description: Per-provider lead overrides. Empty array = every provider uses default_refresh_lead_seconds.

needs_refresh_semantics.description: Normative boolean predicate. A token needs refresh when the current UTC instant plus the (possibly overridden) lead window meets or exceeds expires_at. Reference: forgecode `OAuthTokens::needs_refresh(buffer)` = `Utc::now() + buffer >= self.expires_at`.

is_expired_semantics.description: Normative boolean predicate for hard expiry (lead = 0). Reference: forgecode `OAuthTokens::is_expired()` = `Utc::now() >= self.expires_at`.

non_refreshable_kinds.description: Credential kinds for which needs_refresh always returns false because they are not OAuth tokens (static API keys) or are refreshed by an external SDK (AWS profile, managed by the AWS SDK). Reference: forgecode `AuthCredential::needs_refresh` short-circuits ApiKey and AwsProfile to false.

always_refresh_kinds.description: Credential kinds that should always be treated as needing refresh because they are short-lived (e.g. Google ADC ~1h tokens are re-minted on every check). Reference: forgecode `AuthCredential::needs_refresh` returns true for GoogleAdc.

tokens.description: The token-field model that implementations persist and reason about.
